changesProposed.name: Dawn Aerospace

current.name: Dawn Aerospace
current.founder: Stefan Powell, Jeroen Wink, James Powell, Robert Werner, Tobias Knop

verified.name: Dawn Aerospace
verified.description: Space transportation company building non-toxic, refuelable satellite propulsion systems and a reusable uncrewed suborbital spaceplane (Aurora). The company manufactures chemical propulsion systems for satellites and is developing in-space refueling capabilities.
verified.location: Delft, Netherlands and Christchurch, New Zealand
verified.notes: Company operates from four global locations (Netherlands, New Zealand, France, USA) as of August 2025. The Aurora spaceplane achieved supersonic flight on November 12, 2024, reaching Mach 1.1 and 82,000 ft altitude. Wikipedia lists 120+ employees (August 2025); company website states 130+ people. Current stage not explicitly stated in sources.

changesProposed.name: ICON

current.name: ICON
current.founder: Jason Ballard, Alex Le Roux, Evan Loomis

verified.notes: Sources describe two different companies both named 'Icon': (1) Icon the Human Admaker - a UGC ad creation platform founded by Kennan Frost (previously founded Skio); (2) ICON - a 3D concrete printing technology company mentioned in Wikipedia disambiguation page. The provided database entry appears to describe the 3D-printed homes company, but the sources provided (0-1) only document the UGC ad platform. Source [2] Wikipedia lists 'ICON, a 3D concrete printing technology company' but provides no details. Source [3] is about Solar Foods, not Icon. Unable to verify the database entry claims about 3D-printed homes, DoD/NASA contracts, $450M raised, $2B valuation, or the founders Jason Ballard, Alex Le Roux, and Evan Loomis from these sources.

changesProposed.name: Rivian

verified.name: Rivian Automotive, Inc.
verified.description: American electric vehicle manufacturer founded in 2009 that produces an electric sport utility vehicle (SUV), a pickup truck (R1T), and an electric delivery van on a skateboard platform.
verified.notes: Founded in Rockledge, Florida in 2009 as Mainstream Motors, renamed to Avera Automotive, then Rivian Automotive in 2011. Company went public on Nasdaq (RIVN) on November 10, 2021. Wikipedia article states the company raised 'over US$13.5 billion in financing following its initial public offering in November 2021.' Valuation at IPO was $66.5 billion at offering price but $100 billion on first trading day; no current verified valuation provided in sources.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American spaceflight, telecommunications, and artificial intelligence company that conducts more orbital launches annually than any other launch provider and operates Starlink, a communications satellite company.
verified.notes: Wikipedia indicates SpaceX completed its IPO on June 12, 2026, raising $86 billion. The current database entry claims an IPO valuation of $1.5T and stage of 'IPO' (preparing), but Wikipedia states the IPO already occurred and was the largest in history. No recent valuation figure found in sources to verify the $1.5T or $800B claims. Database entry about '165 Falcon 9 launches in 2025' and '81% of all mass' cannot be verified from these sources.

changesProposed.name: Varda Space Industries

verified.name: Varda Space Industries
verified.description: American privately held space research company that designs, builds, and flies spacecraft to process pharmaceuticals in microgravity and return them to Earth via reentry capsules.
verified.notes: Wikipedia indicates $42M Series A in July 2021 and $90M Series B in April 2024, totaling $131M verified. Database entry claims $187M Series C, but sources only document Series B. No Series C funding found in provided sources. Database claim of 5 missions in 2025 partially verified: W-2 (January 2025), W-3 (March 2025), W-4 (June 2025) confirmed in Wikipedia, but only 3 missions explicitly detailed.
